factor_summary.rationale: Verified public sighting reports near the mission area increase search priority.
Human activity level affects search urgency and area selection.
Crowding/exposure can raise surveillance priority only when paired with sightings, activity context, recent interactions, or environmental signals.
Activity context shapes where a safety team may search; spearfishing is context, not automatic provocation.
Regional SST species context can support surveillance review without dominating priority.
Nearest regional profile and suspected species context adjust search priority.

species_metadata.scoring_note: Probable species is retained as evidence metadata and does not add direct scoring points by itself.

no_sighting_semantics: No-sighting patrol result reduces uncertainty only within documented coverage/time/visibility and is not proof of safety.
